checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=290938000 (2010-12-31) growing to PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=604119000 (2016-12-31), consistent with a capital-intensive LTL trucking business expanding terminal/fleet capacity

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2016 Cash Flow Statement (prior year comparative)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or CostOfGoodsSold concept provided in the dataset (trucking/LTL carrier, minimal inventory expected)

As a proxy, OperatingIncomeLoss=79136000 (2016 annual) / SalesRevenueServicesNet=1218481000 (2016 annual) ~ 6.5% margin vs OperatingIncomeLoss=89975000 (2015 annual) / SalesRevenueServicesNet=1221311000 (2015 annual) ~ 7.4% margin, a modest margin compression consistent with a soft freight market, not an anomalous trend

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss declined from 55016000 (2015 annual) to 48024000 (2016 annual), a -12.7% decline, while SalesRevenueServicesNet was nearly flat, declining from 1221311000 (2015) to 1218481000 (2016), only -0.23%

NetIncomeLoss grew from 1957000 (2010 annual) to 11373000 (2011 annual) to 32048000 (2012 annual) to 43627000 (2013 annual), tracking substantial swings in OperatingIncomeLoss (12100000 in 2010 to 58734000 in 2012)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=45149000 (2016-12-31) vs AccountsPayableCurrent=54754000 (2015-12-31), a decline of about 17.5%, while SalesRevenueServicesNet was roughly flat (1218481000 in 2016 vs 1221311000 in 2015)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: UPLOAD filing dated 2012-08-20, CORRESP filing dated 2012-08-31, UPLOAD filing dated 2012-09-10 (indicative of SEC comment letter correspondence)
UPLOAD filing dated 2016-06-30, CORRESP filing dated 2016-07-14, UPLOAD filing dated 2016-08-02 (indicative of SEC comment letter correspondence)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spanning fiscal years 2008 through 2016 for Assets, AssetsCurrent, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, AccountsPayableCurrent, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingIncomeLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet/Gross, Goodwill, StockholdersEquity and related concepts are provided
Data gaps exist for InventoryNet, CostOfGoodsSold, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivable, and DeferredRevenue, limiting granularity for CL4 and reserve-specific analysis

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew 8.7% (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=135083000 in 2016 vs 124222000 in 2015) while revenue declined slightly (SalesRevenueServicesNet=1218481000 in 2016 vs 1221311000 in 2015), a modest but notable divergence in the most recent fiscal year
Accounts payable declined 17.5% (AccountsPayableCurrent=45149000 in 2016 vs 54754000 in 2015) despite roughly flat revenue, which could reflect faster vendor payment or understated liabilities

SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD filings appear in both 2012 and 2016, suggesting periods of SEC comment-letter review, though no resulting restatements, NT filings, or 10-K/A amendments are evident in the chronology

Operating cash flow consistently and substantially exceeds net income across all years (e.g., NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=145776000 vs NetIncomeLoss=48024000 in 2016), a quality-supportive sign given the capital-intensive trucking business model with high depreciation (Depreciation=74500000 in 2016)
